title: skip_string_b comment misrepresents surrogate handling
description: Comment says 'Any following low-surrogate \uXXXX is handled on the next iteration' — this sounds like it validates them. It doesn't. On the next iteration the \uDCXX sequence is skipped as another 5-byte \uXXXX without surrogate pair checking. The comment should say: 'surrogate pairs are not validated in this path; invalid surrogate sequences pass through without error.' Otherwise a reader thinks validation is deferred to the next iteration.
